title: Reliable Medical Oxygen Systems In Low- And Middle-Income Hospitals
summary: Measure whether health facilities can deliver quality-assured oxygen continuously, distinguishing equipment presence from functional, powered, maintained, and patient-deliverable service reliability.
why_it_matters: A ministry of health, Global Fund, Unitaid, or bilateral funder deciding whether to finance cylinders, concentrators, plants, power backup, distribution, or maintenance cannot allocate well from a simple equipment-inventory count. Verified facility-level uptime and component failure data could change which bottleneck receives the next dollar, while preventing procurement of equipment that cannot be powered, maintained, or used safely.

success_metrics.target: A reviewer can distinguish reliable, functional-but-intermittent, present-but-nonfunctional, and absent oxygen systems at facility level.
success_metrics.validation_method: Cross-check each classification against dated facility assessment fields for source, distribution, delivery, monitoring, power, and maintenance components.

success_metrics.target: The first reviewed output identifies a named financing decision and states what the data cannot justify.
success_metrics.validation_method: Domain and field reviewers inspect the decision statement, denominator, geography, and non-use cases before publication.

success_metrics.target: A replicator can reproduce the classification from the cited assessment instruments and documented rules.
success_metrics.validation_method: Rerun the classification on a sample of facility records and compare exclusions, missingness, and component-level results.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, oxygen-system domain review, field-reality and red-team review, and independent replication before any financing or service recommendation.
